title: StudioDash Reporting
description: Review trends, compare performance, and understand your developer data with StudioDash reporting.
summary: StudioDash reporting helps you understand what changed, why it changed, and where to focus your attention next.

featureShowcases.heading: Understand Your Results
featureShowcases.summary: Review your apps, revenue, websites, and connected services with reports designed to highlight the information that matters most.
featureShowcases.body: StudioDash helps you move beyond individual metrics by bringing related information together. Compare time periods, identify trends, and quickly understand what has changed across your portfolio without manually rebuilding reports every time.
bullets: Review historical performance across connected providers.
Compare apps, revenue, websites, and provider data together.
Move naturally from summary reports into detailed analytics.

image.caption: Understand trends across your entire portfolio.

sections.heading: Reporting That Helps You Take Action
paragraphs: StudioDash reporting is designed to answer the questions developers ask every day: What changed? Which app or service was affected? Where should I focus next?
Instead of manually combining information from multiple dashboards, StudioDash helps you review everything from one connected reporting experience.

sections.heading: Reporting Across Your Portfolio
paragraphs: StudioDash combines information from your dashboard, revenue analytics, App Store Connect, website analytics, integrations, and provider health into one reporting workflow.
Historical trends sit alongside current operational information, helping you understand both long-term performance and what's happening right now.
bullets: Revenue breakdowns by app, provider, country, and date.
Clear explanations when data is unavailable or still being configured.

sections.heading: Getting the Most from Your Reports
paragraphs: Start by selecting the reporting period you want to review, then compare the trends across your connected apps and services.
If a metric is missing, StudioDash helps explain whether the reason is unavailable provider data, a configuration issue, or simply that no activity occurred during the selected period.
bullets: Choose the reporting period before comparing results.
Review provider health alongside performance data.
Use app, provider, country, and date breakdowns to identify changes.
Understand the difference between live, unavailable, and planned data.

sections.heading: Future Reporting Features
paragraphs: StudioDash will continue expanding its reporting capabilities with exports, scheduled reports, forecasting, intelligent summaries, and portfolio-level insights.
As these features become available, they will help developers spend less time collecting information and more time acting on it.
